messages.content:
I can’t run tasks or execute load tests from here. I can, however, tell you exactly how to run a “load test task 0” locally or on your CI — I just need one quick clarification: what tooling does your project use for load testing (locust, k6, artillery, ab, custom script, or something else), and what does “task 0” refer to (a specific test file, a target endpoint, or a named scenario)?

Brief reasoning: I can’t execute commands remotely, but I can provide exact commands and interpretation steps once I know the tool and target.

Below are concise examples for common tools — pick the one that matches your project or tell me which to tailor the instructions to your repo.

- Locust (Python locustfile):
  - Start system under test (e.g. your app at http://localhost:8000).
  - Run headless: locust -f locustfile.py --headless -u 100 -r 10 -t 1m --host=http://localhost:8000
  - If your locustfile defines named tasks/scenarios, pass an environment variable or configure the file to run “task 0” (you may need to edit locustfile to select scenario 0).

- k6 (JavaScript):
  - Example: k6 run --vus 100 --duration 1m loadtest_task0.js
  - In the script (loadtest_task0.js), ensure the default function or an exported scenario implements “task 0.”

- Artillery (YAML scenario):
  - Run: artillery run artillery-task0.yml
  - Put the scenario for “task 0” in that YAML or use a separate YAML per task.

- ApacheBench (simple HTTP):
  - ab -n 1000 -c 100 http://localhost:8000/your-endpoint

- If your project uses npm scripts or a Makefile:
  - npm run loadtest:task0
  - or make load-test TASK=0
  - (Check package.json or Makefile for exact script name.)

What I can provide next if you tell me which tool and what “task 0” means:
- Exact command(s) to run locally and in CI (including Docker if needed).
- Template locust/k6/artillery script for “task 0” if you don’t have one.
- How to parse results and what performance thresholds to look for.

If you want me to modify or add a load-test script in the repo, submit again with a concrete coding request (or turn off Chat-only mode).

nextActions.description: Ask a follow-up question in the same thread.
